Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First, compute case durations: from first event (Submit Claim) to last event (Close Claim). Lets extract times. Case 2001: Submit: 2024-04-01 09:00 Close: 2024-04-01 10:30 => duration 1.5 hours (90 minutes). Very short. Case 2002: Submit: 2024-04-01 09:05 Close: 2024-04-02 11:00 => about 1 day 1 hour 55 min. So ~26 hours. Case 2003: Submit: 2024-04-01 09:10 Close: 2024-04-03 09:30 => ~48.33 hours. Case 2004: Submit: 2024-04-01 09:20 Close: 2024-04-01 10:45 => 1 hour 25 min (85 minutes). Very short. Case 2005: Submit: 2024-04-01 09:25 Close: 2024-04-04 14:30 => total 77h5m.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So long-duration cases: 2002 (~26h), 2003 (~48h), 2005 (~77h). Case 2001 and 2004 are short (under 2h).

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now analyze attributes: Case 2001: Region A, Complexity Low, Resources: CSR_Jane, Adjuster_Mike, Manager_Ann, Finance_Alan. Case 2002: Region B, Complexity Medium, Resources: CSR_Paul, Adjuster_Lisa, Manager_Ann, Finance_Carl. Case 2003: Region A, Complexity High, Resources: CSR_Jane, Adjuster_Mike, Manager_Bill, Finance_Alan. Case 2004: Region B, Complexity Low, Resources: CSR_Mary, Adjuster_Lisa, Manager_Ann, Finance_Carl. Case 2005: Region B, Complexity High, Resources: CSR_Paul, Adjuster_Lisa, Manager_Bill, Finance_Carl.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Observations: Low complexity cases (2001, 2004) are fast. They have no Request Additional Documents steps. They proceed straight from Evaluate to Approve. Medium complexity (2002) has one Request Additional Documents step and takes about 1 day. High complexity (2003 and 2005) have multiple Request Additional Documents. 2003 has two such steps, 2005 has three steps. So complexity level directly correlates with number of additional document requests, which extends duration.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region: Both A and B have fast low-complexity cases. But high complexity in Region A (2003) took ~2 days, while high complexity in Region B (2005) took ~3 days. Could be due to resource differences? Resources: Adjuster_Mike handled 2001 (low) and 2003 (high). Adjuster_Lisa handled 2002 (medium), 2004 (low), and 2005 (high). In 2005, she requested docs three times over several days. Possibly Adjuster_Lisa tends to have more delays or the complexity of claims in Region B leads to more requests?

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the time between last request and approval: In 2003, last request on 04-01 17:00, approval on 04-02 16:00 => 23h. In 2005, last request on 04-03 15:00, approval on 04-04 10:00 => 19h. For 2005, gap from 04-01 11:30 to 04-02 17:00 (over a day) for second request, then to 04-03 15:00 (another day). So the intervals between requests are much longer in 2005.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Root causes: Complexity is a clear driver: high complexity claims require multiple additional document requests, which increases duration significantly. The number of requests and the time between them cause delays. Resource: Adjuster_Lisa seems to have longer intervals between requests in high complexity (2005) compared to Adjuster_Mike (2003). Region: Region B might have more challenging claims or slower response from customers.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Mitigation suggestions: For high complexity claims, set clear timelines for each additional document request and follow-up. Consider assigning dedicated resources for complex claims. Standardize the process for requesting documents to reduce delays. Monitor adjuster performance and provide training if needed. Implement a system to track document submission deadlines.
